summary:
I have investigated the codebase and found the "needle" – the bug was a classic lock-ordering deadlock in the `TransferService.java` file. 

When transferring funds concurrently in opposite directions (e.g., A to B, and B to A), threads would lock the accounts in parameter order. A thread processing A to B locks A then B, while another processing B to A locks B then A, causing a deadlock. 

I fixed this by enforcing a global lock ordering rule using `Account.getId().compareTo(...)` to ensure that locks are always acquired in the same order regardless of the transfer direction. I also updated the `completedTransfers` and `failedTransfers` counters to use `AtomicInteger`, preventing any race conditions on those stats during concurrent transfers.

Running `/app/test.sh` confirms the fix:
```
Running 200 concurrent transfers across 8 threads with 10s timeout...
...
PASS: All concurrent transfers completed without deadlock
```
